betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The Pattern Repeater is a niche but useful tool. It allows recipes to be visible across networks.

When two networks are linked via a repeater, you could have a pattern that lives on network A and be able to see it in network B. In practice, you could request the item from network B (using items from network B), then those items would be transferred to the pattern destination on network A, and the resulting item would end up on network B when completed.

In order to set this up, simply make a pair of Pattern Repeaters (attached to separate networks) face into one another. You can then use a wrench to toggle each to the desired mode: either Provider or Accessor. You will need one of each.

The "§5Accessor§r" side is where the original pattern should sit. It will "send" the pattern to the other network. §3(Network A in the example above)§r

The "§5Provider§r" side is where the pattern is provided to. It "receives" the incoming pattern from the other network. §3(Network B in the example above)§r
